clusters.title: Xi Warns Trump About Taiwan
clusters.neutral_headline: Xi warns Trump of ‘clashes and even conflicts’ with US over Taiwan
bullet_summary: Xi’s remarks, published by China’s foreign ministry after his two-hour meeting with Trump on Thursday morning, said Taiwan was “the most important issue in China-US relations”
The Chinese Foreign ministry said the comments came in a closed-door meeting that lasted more than two hours

items.title: Xi Warns Trump About Taiwan
items.standfirst: Xi Jinping warned Donald Trump that Taiwan could lead to "clashes" between the superpowers, calling it a "highly dangerous situation" for the world's biggest economies. Michelle Giuda, CEO of the Krach Institute for Tech Diplomacy at Purdue, joins Ed Ludlow on "Bloomberg Tech" to discuss what this means for the US tech sector and what else transpired from the meeting between the two leaders. (Source: Bloomberg)
items.content: Xi Jinping warned Donald Trump that Taiwan could lead to "clashes" between the superpowers, calling it a "highly dangerous situation" for the world's biggest economies. Michelle Giuda, CEO of the Krach Institute for Tech Diplomacy at Purdue, joins Ed Ludlow on "Bloomberg Tech" to discuss what this means for the US tech sector and what else transpired from the meeting between the two leaders. (Source: Bloomberg)

Continue reading...

items.title: 'Extremely positive and productive talks': Trump praises Xi meeting
items.standfirst: Chinese President Xi Jinping warned his US counterpart Donald Trump that missteps on Taiwan could push their two countries into "conflict", a stark opening salvo as they met in Beijing on Thursday for a two-day summit. The Chinese Foreign ministry said the comments came in a closed-door meeting that lasted more than two hours. It was followed by a friendlier, opulent banquet during which Trump and Xi stressed the importance of working together. Story by Carys Garland.
items.content: Chinese President Xi Jinping warned his US counterpart Donald Trump that missteps on Taiwan could push their two countries into "conflict", a stark opening salvo as they met in Beijing on Thursday for a two-day summit. The Chinese Foreign ministry said the comments came in a closed-door meeting that lasted more than two hours. It was followed by a friendlier, opulent banquet during which Trump and Xi stressed the importance of working together. Story by Carys Garland.

items.title: US-China relations depend on Taiwan, Xi warns Trump

items.content: Chinese president tells US counterpart there are ‘no winners’ in trade war

items.title: In pictures: Trump hosted by Xi Jinping in Beijing on two-day summit
items.standfirst: President Trump was welcomed by President Xi Jinping in Beijing for what will be a whirlwind tour.
items.content: President Trump was welcomed by President Xi Jinping in Beijing for what will be a whirlwind tour.

items.title: 'Conflict' possible: Xi Jinping issues Taiwan warning during Donald Trump's visit
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump is in Beijing today, meeting China's President Xi Jinping. This visit is the first by a US leader in nearly a decade. Talks so far have focused on trade and Taiwan… with Xi warning that disagreement over the island's future could even lead to conflict. However, the pair also exchanged a cordial welcome and emphasised the links between their two nations. Analysis by FRANCE 24 international affairs editor, Kethevane Gorjestani.
items.content: US President Donald Trump is in Beijing today, meeting China's President Xi Jinping. This visit is the first by a US leader in nearly a decade. Talks so far have focused on trade and Taiwan… with Xi warning that disagreement over the island's future could even lead to conflict. However, the pair also exchanged a cordial welcome and emphasised the links between their two nations. Analysis by FRANCE 24 international affairs editor, Kethevane Gorjestani.

items.title: Xi warns Trump of possible conflict over Taiwan at grand Beijing summit
items.standfirst: BEIJING — Chinese President Xi Jinping warned Thursday of “clashes and even conflicts” with the United States over Taiwan, cautioning President Donald Trump that tensions over the Beijing-claimed island could jeopardize ties between the world’s two biggest economies
items.content: BEIJING — Chinese President Xi Jinping warned Thursday of “clashes and even conflicts” with the United States over Taiwan, cautioning President Donald Trump that tensions over the Beijing-claimed island could jeopardize ties between the world’s two biggest economies

items.title: Watch: What happened on day one of Trump's China visit?
items.standfirst: China welcomed US President Donald Trump with cheering children and a troop parade on Thursday, before a nearly two-hour long meeting with Xi-Jinping.
items.content: China welcomed US President Donald Trump with cheering children and a troop parade on Thursday, before a nearly two-hour long meeting with Xi-Jinping.

items.title: President Trump visits Temple of Heaven following historic U.S.-China summit
items.standfirst: President Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping visited the Temple of Heaven after their two-hour bilateral meeting. NBC News’ Garrett Haake and Brian Cheung report on the key issues discussed and the leaders’ cultural visit to the Temple of Heaven.
items.content: President Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping visited the Temple of Heaven after their two-hour bilateral meeting. NBC News’ Garrett Haake and Brian Cheung report on the key issues discussed and the leaders’ cultural visit to the Temple of Heaven.

items.title: Trump shakes hands with Xi at start of historic U.S.-China summit
items.standfirst: President Trump arrived at the Great Hall of the People to meet with Chinese President Xi Jinping. Trump greeted several senior Chinese officials, while President Xi welcomed top U.S. officials.
items.content: President Trump arrived at the Great Hall of the People to meet with Chinese President Xi Jinping. Trump greeted several senior Chinese officials, while President Xi welcomed top U.S. officials.

items.title: Trade, Taiwan and Iran cast shadows on Trump’s China summit with Xi
items.standfirst: President Donald Trump will begin a two-day summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping in Beijing on Thursday as the two countries engage in a fierce trade war and the U.S. conflict with Iran rages on.
items.content: President Donald Trump will begin a two-day summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping in Beijing on Thursday as the two countries engage in a fierce trade war and the U.S. conflict with Iran rages on.

clusters.title: Latvian PM resigns over handling of stray Ukrainian drones
clusters.neutral_headline: Latvian PM resigns over handling of stray Ukrainian drones
bullet_summary: Reported by Al Jazeera, France24, Guardian World and others

items.title: Latvian PM resigns over handling of stray Ukrainian drones
items.standfirst: Latvia’s Prime Minister Evika Silina resigned following criticism of her government’s handling of stray drones
items.content: Latvia’s Prime Minister Evika Silina resigned following criticism of her government’s handling of stray drones

items.title: How stray Ukrainian drones pushed Latvia's prime minister to resign
items.standfirst: Latvian Prime Minister Evika Silina resigned Thursday after a key party in her coalition withdrew its support for her sacking of the defence minister after Ukrainian drones repeatedly strayed into the country. Silina had blamed defence minister Andris Spruds for the incursions, saying the country's anti-drone systems had not been deployed quickly enough.
items.content: Latvian Prime Minister Evika Silina resigned Thursday after a key party in her coalition withdrew its support for her sacking of the defence minister after Ukrainian drones repeatedly strayed into the country. Silina had blamed defence minister Andris Spruds for the incursions, saying the country's anti-drone systems had not been deployed quickly enough.

items.title: Latvian prime minister resigns amid row over drone incursions
items.standfirst: Evika Siliņa stands down after coalition collapses following sacking of defence ministerLatvia’s centre-right prime minister has resigned over her government’s handling of Ukrainian drones that strayed into Latvian territory from Russia, bringing down her coalition government months before elections due in October.Evika Siliņa announced her resignation on Thursday, a day after the Progressives party, her left-leaning coalition partner, withdrew its support over her decision to fire the defence minister, Andris Sprūds, a Progressives member. Continue reading...
items.content: Evika Siliņa stands down after coalition collapses following sacking of defence ministerLatvia’s centre-right prime minister has resigned over her government’s handling of Ukrainian drones that strayed into Latvian territory from Russia, bringing down her coalition government months before elections due in October.Evika Siliņa announced her resignation on Thursday, a day after the Progressives party, her left-leaning coalition partner, withdrew its support over her decision to fire the defence minister, Andris Sprūds, a Progressives member. Continue reading...

items.content: A ship was taken by unknown parties toward Iranian waters after an Indian-flagged vessel was attacked off Oman.

items.title: Iran says ships entering strait of Hormuz must cooperate after vessel seized
items.standfirst: Docked ship reportedly seized outside UAE port by “unauthorised personnel”The Iranian foreign minister, Abbas Araghchi, has said ships entering the strait of Hormuz must cooperate with the Iranian navy as reports emerged of a ship being seized outside a United Arab Emirate port and taken towards Iranian waters.The UK Maritime Trading Organisation said the docked ship was seized by “unauthorised personnel” while it was anchored off the coast of the United Arab Emirates port of Fujairah near the southern entry to the strait of Hormuz. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Iran accuses United Arab Emirates of being 'active partner' in US-Israeli war
clusters.neutral_headline: Benjamin Netanyahu says he made secret trip to UAE at height of Iran war
bullet_summary: “This visit has led to a historic breakthrough in relations between Israel and the UAE,” the Israeli prime minister’s office said on Wednesday night
Benjamin Netanyahu's office said he secretly visited the United Arab Emirates earlier this year during the US-Israeli war with Iran - but the UAE has denied the trip took place

items.title: Iran accuses United Arab Emirates of being 'active partner' in US-Israeli war
items.standfirst: Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi accused the United Arab Emirates on Thursday of playing an active role in the US-Israeli war against his country. Araghchi also referred to what Israel has described as a "secret" meeting in the UAE during the war between Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and Emirati President Mohammed bin Zayed Al Nahyan -- a visit Abu Dhabi denied took place. FRANCE 24's Noga Tarnopolsky reports from Jerusalem.
items.content: Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi accused the United Arab Emirates on Thursday of playing an active role in the US-Israeli war against his country. Araghchi also referred to what Israel has described as a "secret" meeting in the UAE during the war between Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and Emirati President Mohammed bin Zayed Al Nahyan -- a visit Abu Dhabi denied took place. FRANCE 24's Noga Tarnopolsky reports from Jerusalem.

items.title: Benjamin Netanyahu says he made secret trip to UAE at height of Iran war
items.standfirst: Emirates’ foreign ministry rejects claims that Netanyahu visited country, describing them as ‘baseless’Benjamin Netanyahu has claimed he made a secret trip to the United Arab Emirates at the height of the Iran war to meet the president, Sheikh Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan.“This visit has led to a historic breakthrough in relations between Israel and the UAE,” the Israeli prime minister’s office said on Wednesday night. Continue reading...
items.content: Emirates’ foreign ministry rejects claims that Netanyahu visited country, describing them as ‘baseless’Benjamin Netanyahu has claimed he made a secret trip to the United Arab Emirates at the height of the Iran war to meet the president, Sheikh Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan.“This visit has led to a historic breakthrough in relations between Israel and the UAE,” the Israeli prime minister’s office said on Wednesday night. Continue reading...

items.title: A Philippine senator wanted by the International Criminal Court flees from Senate
items.standfirst: A police investigation is underway in the Philippines, with suspicions that the incident was staged to help Sen. Ronald dela Rosa escape.
items.content: A police investigation is underway in the Philippines, with suspicions that the incident was staged to help Sen. Ronald dela Rosa escape.(Image credit: Aaron Favila)

items.title: Philippine politician wanted by ICC flees senate after days holed up in building
items.standfirst: Ronald dela Rosa, wanted over involvement in Duterte’s ‘war on drugs’, reportedly left heavily guarded building before dawnA Philippine lawmaker wanted by the international criminal court for his alleged role enforcing Rodrigo Duterte’s bloody anti-drugs crackdown has secretly fled the senate after spending days holed up in the building to avoid arrest.The senate president, Alan Peter Cayetano, confirmed to the media that senator Ronald dela Rosa was “no longer in the building” after reports that he had slipped out of the heavily guarded building before dawn. Continue reading...
items.content: Ronald dela Rosa, wanted over involvement in Duterte’s ‘war on drugs’, reportedly left heavily guarded building before dawnA Philippine lawmaker wanted by the international criminal court for his alleged role enforcing Rodrigo Duterte’s bloody anti-drugs crackdown has secretly fled the senate after spending days holed up in the building to avoid arrest.The senate president, Alan Peter Cayetano, confirmed to the media that senator Ronald dela Rosa was “no longer in the building” after reports that he had slipped out of the heavily guarded building before dawn. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Mbappé heads up France squad in Deschamps' final World Cup bid
clusters.neutral_headline: Mbappe and Dembele head up star-studded France World Cup squad

items.title: Mbappé heads up France squad in Deschamps' final World Cup bid
items.standfirst: Kylian Mbappé will lead France’s bid for World Cup glory after coach Didier Deschamps named a largely unchanged 26-man squad Thursday for this summer’s tournament in the United States, Canada and Mexico. Mbappé, recovering from a thigh injury suffered during Real Madrid’s title run-in, is still expected to captain the team.
items.content: Kylian Mbappé will lead France’s bid for World Cup glory after coach Didier Deschamps named a largely unchanged 26-man squad Thursday for this summer’s tournament in the United States, Canada and Mexico. Mbappé, recovering from a thigh injury suffered during Real Madrid’s title run-in, is still expected to captain the team.

items.title: Mbappe, Dembele head up France squad for 2026 World Cup
items.standfirst: Kylian Mbappe will spearhead France at the World Cup after national team coach Didier Deschamps on Thursday announced his 26-man squad for this summer's tournament in the United States, Canada and Mexico. FRANCE 24's Mark Owen speaks with sports journalist and broadcaster Thando Sibiya.

clusters.title: Labour politicians jockey for possible challenge to UK's Starmer
clusters.neutral_headline: Labour MP to stand down to allow Burnham run for byelection amid leadership row

items.title: Labour politicians jockey for possible challenge to UK's Starmer
items.standfirst: British ​Labour lawmaker Josh Simons on Thursday said he would resign from his seat in parliament in a move ​designed ‌to give Greater Manchester Mayor Andy ⁠Burnham a chance to return to parliament and challenge Prime Minister ‌Keir Starmer. FRANCE 24's Béédicte Paviot reports from 10 Downing Street.
items.content: British ​Labour lawmaker Josh Simons on Thursday said he would resign from his seat in parliament in a move ​designed ‌to give Greater Manchester Mayor Andy ⁠Burnham a chance to return to parliament and challenge Prime Minister ‌Keir Starmer. FRANCE 24's Béédicte Paviot reports from 10 Downing Street.

items.title: Labour MP to stand down to allow Burnham run for byelection amid leadership row
items.standfirst: Greater Manchester mayor would need to win Josh Simons’s former seat to launch challenge against Starmer UK politics live – latest updatesAndy Burnham now has a route back to parliament after the Labour MP for Makerfield announced he would stand down, triggering a byelection, and putting the senior politician in contention to become the party’s next leader.The Greater Manchester mayor confirmed on Thursday he would be asking Labour’s ruling national executive committee to allow him to stand in the contest, putting a beleaguered Keir Starmer under intense pressure to not try to block him. Continue reading...
items.content: Greater Manchester mayor would need to win Josh Simons’s former seat to launch challenge against Starmer UK politics live – latest updatesAndy Burnham now has a route back to parliament after the Labour MP for Makerfield announced he would stand down, triggering a byelection, and putting the senior politician in contention to become the party’s next leader.The Greater Manchester mayor confirmed on Thursday he would be asking Labour’s ruling national executive committee to allow him to stand in the contest, putting a beleaguered Keir Starmer under intense pressure to not try to block him. Continue reading...

clusters.title: US House leaders announce bipartisan effort to combat sexual misconduct in Congress
clusters.neutral_headline: House leaders to take action on process for sexual misconduct claims
bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian World, CBS News

items.title: US House leaders announce bipartisan effort to combat sexual misconduct in Congress
items.standfirst: Announcement of taskforce comes after resignations of two congressmen amid sexual misconduct allegationsUS politics live – latest updatesThe House speaker, Mike Johnson, and the minority leader, Hakeem Jeffries, on Wednesday announced a bipartisan effort to combat sexual misconduct on Capitol Hill.The “partnership” led by the chairs of the Republican and Democratic women’s caucuses – congresswomen Kat Cammack of Florida and Teresa Leger Fernández of New Mexico – aims to “identify reforms and solutions to make Congress a safer work environment for women and all survivors”, the leaders said in a joint statement. Continue reading...
items.content: Announcement of taskforce comes after resignations of two congressmen amid sexual misconduct allegationsUS politics live – latest updatesThe House speaker, Mike Johnson, and the minority leader, Hakeem Jeffries, on Wednesday announced a bipartisan effort to combat sexual misconduct on Capitol Hill.The “partnership” led by the chairs of the Republican and Democratic women’s caucuses – congresswomen Kat Cammack of Florida and Teresa Leger Fernández of New Mexico – aims to “identify reforms and solutions to make Congress a safer work environment for women and all survivors”, the leaders said in a joint statement. Continue reading...

items.title: House leaders to take action on process for sexual misconduct claims
items.standfirst: Speaker Mike Johnson and Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries are launching a bipartisan task force aimed at addressing how sexual misconduct claims are handled within the House of Representatives, multiple sources confirm to CBS News.
items.content: Speaker Mike Johnson and Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries are launching a bipartisan task force aimed at addressing how sexual misconduct claims are handled within the House of Representatives, multiple sources confirm to CBS News.

items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump on Thursday said President Xi Jinping had offered China's help to open the Strait of Hormuz and pledged not to send military equipment to aid Iran in its war against the US and Israel.
items.content: US President Donald Trump on Thursday said President Xi Jinping had offered China's help to open the Strait of Hormuz and pledged not to send military equipment to aid Iran in its war against the US and Israel.

clusters.title: Israel says it will sue New York Times over article on sexual abuse of Palestinian prisoners
clusters.neutral_headline: Israel’s Netanyahu says suing New York Times over Palestinian rape article
bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian Business, Al Jazeera

items.title: Israel says it will sue New York Times over article on sexual abuse of Palestinian prisoners
items.standfirst: Media law experts cast doubt on viability of a defamation lawsuit promised by Netanyahu over Nicholas Kristof essayIsrael’s prime minister, Benjamin Nethanyahu, and foreign minister, Gideon Sa’ar, have threatened to sue the New York Times for defamation over the publication of an essay by Nicholas Kristof detailing allegations that Palestinian women, men and children have been raped and sexually abused in Israeli military detention.“Following the publication by Nicholas Kristof in The New York Times of one of the most hideous and distorted lies ever published against the State of Israel in the modern press, which also received the backing of the newspaper, Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and Foreign Minister Gideon Sa’ar have instructed the initiation of a defamation lawsuit against The New York Times,” Israel’s ministry of foreign affairs wrote in a social media post on Thursday. Continue reading...
items.content: Media law experts cast doubt on viability of a defamation lawsuit promised by Netanyahu over Nicholas Kristof essayIsrael’s prime minister, Benjamin Nethanyahu, and foreign minister, Gideon Sa’ar, have threatened to sue the New York Times for defamation over the publication of an essay by Nicholas Kristof detailing allegations that Palestinian women, men and children have been raped and sexually abused in Israeli military detention.“Following the publication by Nicholas Kristof in The New York Times of one of the most hideous and distorted lies ever published against the State of Israel in the modern press, which also received the backing of the newspaper, Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and Foreign Minister Gideon Sa’ar have instructed the initiation of a defamation lawsuit against The New York Times,” Israel’s ministry of foreign affairs wrote in a social media post on Thursday. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Body of second U.S. soldier who went missing in Morocco recovered
clusters.neutral_headline: Remains of second US soldier who went missing in Morocco recovered
bullet_summary: The soldier was identified as Spc Mariyah Symone Collington of Taveres, Florida, the US Army Europe and Africa said in a statement
Reported by CBS News, Guardian World

items.title: Body of second U.S. soldier who went missing in Morocco recovered
items.standfirst: The Army identified the soldier as Spc. Mariyah Collington.
items.content: The Army identified the soldier as Spc. Mariyah Collington.

items.title: Remains of second US soldier who went missing in Morocco recovered
items.standfirst: Mariyah Symone Collington and Kendrick Lamont Key Jr, who also died, had fallen off a cliff during an off-duty hikeThe remains of the second US army soldier who went missing during military exercises in Morocco have been recovered, the army said on Wednesday, ending a multinational search operation that deployed air, naval and artificial intelligence assets.The soldier was identified as Spc Mariyah Symone Collington of Taveres, Florida, the US Army Europe and Africa said in a statement. She was 19 years old. Continue reading...
items.content: Mariyah Symone Collington and Kendrick Lamont Key Jr, who also died, had fallen off a cliff during an off-duty hikeThe remains of the second US army soldier who went missing during military exercises in Morocco have been recovered, the army said on Wednesday, ending a multinational search operation that deployed air, naval and artificial intelligence assets.The soldier was identified as Spc Mariyah Symone Collington of Taveres, Florida, the US Army Europe and Africa said in a statement. She was 19 years old. Continue reading...
